markdownRemark.html:
I sometimes get questions through DMs or emails mainly from design students. These one-on-one conversations get lost over time, and I find myself having to repeat the same answers over again. Instead, I am adding answers to common questions here. This post may be updated later.

Techniques

First of all, there have been many questions about techniques. Here is a general suggestion for you. Do your own research first. Most artists/designers link their portfolio website. Go there and see what they have been making. You may find helpful information or keywords there. You can also get a lot of answers just by looking at the hashtags of the posts, and previous conversations with other people. If you can't find the answers even after doing your research, then, maybe ask the artist directly. If they know that you've done your part, they may be more willing to take their time for you.

What softwares do you use?

All my personal works are created with code. No Adobe softwares except for retouching and editing. I use the JavaScript programming language (and sometimes, GLSL shading language) to create almost all my work.

What programming language/libraries do you use?

I use a JavaScript library called p5.js to prototype my sketches.

Do you use TouchDesigner?

No. I use JavaScript.

Can I see your source code?

I don't usually share my source code because I am constantly iterating and it's not very well organized. And I don't think there is something very special about my techniques that you cannot find elsewhere on the internet.

Learning

Many people wonder what the best way to learn to code is. Well, I don't think there is one solution that fits all. But I can tell you one thing for certain. You need to just start. Just do it. Take a few hours this weekend, find a very easy tutorial on Youtube, follow along, and just make something. You will learn much more by doing it than reading or watching alone. If you can afford, sign up for a paid course. This may give you a better motivation.

Can I ask you programming questions?

Unfortunately no. My time and resources are limited so I cannot help you personally. Here are a few suggestions for you, though. Google search is your friend. Professional programmers also find their answers this way. You need to have patience to go through many scattered information online and filter through what you need. If you are not used to, this is a very good skill to develop. Second, use Github. There are many open source code examples. You just need to use the right search keywords. Also, follow people on social media, especially those who share their code and process.

Do you have tutorials?

If you are a beginner, I highly recommend you start your coding journey with Coding Train Youtube channel by Daniel Shiffman.

How should I go about learning to code?

There are many different programming languages and you might be wondering which one you should start with for the things that you want to do. My advice - do not worry if you are a beginner. Just pick any language and learn the basics. You can usually transfer the basic skills from one language to another pretty easily once you feel comfortable. My recommendation is to start with p5js JavaScript library.
